payload.headline: Live macro momentum is leaning soft for prices (score -0.79 on a -2 to +2 scale).
lines: Live macro momentum is leaning soft for prices (score -0.79 on a -2 to +2 scale).
2-year fixed mortgage rate is 4.84%, above its 3-year average of 4.76% - a drag on affordability.
Real pay growth is about +0.9% (pay 4.1% against 2.8% inflation); rising real incomes lift buyer budgets.
Unemployment is 5.0%, above its 3-year average of 4.5% - softening labour market underpins less of housing demand.
These are demand conditions over the next 6 to 12 months. They sit beside the figure, not inside it - the number is built from sold evidence.
